questions.q: What is the primary function of the cell membrane?
opts: (A) To provide structural support to the cell
(B) To control the movement of materials in and out of the cell
(C) To synthesize proteins and ribosomal RNA
(D) To store genetic information in the form of DNA
questions.explanation: The cell membrane, also known as the plasma membrane, is a thin semi-permeable lipid bilayer that surrounds the cell. It controls the movement of materials in and out of the cell through various transport mechanisms.

questions.q: What is the main difference between a prokaryotic and eukaryotic cell?
opts: (A) Presence of a nucleus in prokaryotic cells
(B) Absence of a true nucleus in prokaryotic cells
(C) Presence of mitochondria in prokaryotic cells
(D) Absence of ribosomes in prokaryotic cells
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic cells, such as bacteria, lack a true nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles. In contrast, eukaryotic cells, such as animal cells, have a true nucleus and other organelles.

questions.q: Which type of connective tissue is characterized by a loose arrangement of cells and fibers?
opts: (A) Bone tissue
(B) Cartilage tissue
(C) Adipose tissue
(D) Areolar tissue
questions.explanation: Areolar tissue is a type of loose connective tissue that is found in the skin, subcutaneous tissue, and other parts of the body. It is composed of a loose arrangement of cells and fibers.

questions.q: What is the function of the Golgi apparatus in a cell?
opts: (A) To synthesize proteins and ribosomal RNA
(B) To store genetic information in the form of DNA
(C) To package and modify proteins and lipids
(D) To provide structural support to the cell
questions.explanation: The Golgi apparatus is a complex organelle that is responsible for modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins and lipids for transport to other parts of the cell or for secretion.

questions.q: What is the primary function of the mitochondria in a cell?
opts: (A) To synthesize proteins and ribosomal RNA
(B) To store genetic information in the form of DNA
(C) To generate energy for the cell through cellular respiration
(D) To provide structural support to the cell
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouses of the cell, responsible for generating energy for the cell through cellular respiration.

questions.q: What is the structure of a muscle fiber?
opts: (A) A single muscle cell with multiple nuclei
(B) A group of muscle cells with a common basement membrane
(C) A single muscle cell with a single nucleus
(D) A group of muscle cells with multiple nuclei
questions.explanation: A muscle fiber is a single muscle cell that is multinucleated, meaning it has multiple nuclei. Each muscle fiber is composed of many myofibrils, which are the contractile units of the muscle.

questions.q: What is the difference between a tendon and a ligament?
opts: (A) A tendon connects a muscle to a bone, while a ligament connects a bone to a bone
(B) A tendon connects a bone to a bone, while a ligament connects a muscle to a bone
(C) A tendon is a type of connective tissue that is found in the skin, while a ligament is a type of connective tissue that is found in the joints
(D) A tendon is a type of connective tissue that is found in the joints, while a ligament is a type of connective tissue that is found in the skin
questions.explanation: Tendons connect muscles to bones, while ligaments connect bones to bones. This is a key difference between the two types of connective tissue.

questions.q: What is the primary function of the nervous system?
opts: (A) To control the movement of materials in and out of the cell
(B) To synthesize proteins and ribosomal RNA
(C) To transmit and process information
(D) To store genetic information in the form of DNA
questions.explanation: The nervous system is responsible for transmitting and processing information throughout the body. It consists of the brain, spinal cord, and nerves.

questions.q: What is the structure of the eye?
opts: (A) The eye is a simple lens that focuses light on the retina
(B) The eye is a complex organ that consists of the cornea, iris, lens, retina, and optic nerve
(C) The eye is a type of connective tissue that is found in the head
(D) The eye is a type of muscle that is found in the face
questions.explanation: The eye is a complex organ that consists of several parts, including the cornea, iris, lens, retina, and optic nerve. Each of these parts plays a critical role in the process of vision.

questions.q: What is the function of the olfactory epithelium?
opts: (A) To synthesize proteins and ribosomal RNA
(B) To store genetic information in the form of DNA
(C) To detect odor molecules
(D) To provide structural support to the cell
questions.explanation: The olfactory epithelium is a specialized tissue in the nose that is responsible for detecting odor molecules. It is composed of sensory receptors that are sensitive to different types of odors.

questions.q: What is the primary function of the pancreas?
opts: (A) To synthesize proteins and ribosomal RNA
(B) To store genetic information in the form of DNA
(C) To regulate blood sugar levels
(D) To provide structural support to the cell
questions.explanation: The pancreas is a gland that is responsible for regulating blood sugar levels. It produces hormones such as insulin and glucagon that help to control glucose metabolism.

questions.q: What is the structure of the skin?
opts: (A) The skin is a simple layer of epithelial cells
(B) The skin is a complex organ that consists of several layers, including the epidermis, dermis, and hypodermis
(C) The skin is a type of connective tissue that is found in the head
(D) The skin is a type of muscle that is found in the face
questions.explanation: The skin is a complex organ that consists of several layers, including the epidermis, dermis, and hypodermis. Each of these layers plays a critical role in the process of maintaining the body's integrity.

questions.q: What is the primary function of the skeletal system?
opts: (A) To control the movement of materials in and out of the cell
(B) To synthesize proteins and ribosomal RNA
(C) To provide support and protection for the body
(D) To store genetic information in the form of DNA
questions.explanation: The skeletal system is responsible for providing support and protection for the body. It consists of bones, cartilage, and ligaments that work together to maintain the body's structure.

questions.q: What is the difference between a histone and a non-histone protein?
opts: (A) A histone is a type of non-histone protein that is found in the nucleus
(B) A histone is a type of protein that is involved in DNA replication, while a non-histone protein is involved in transcription
(C) A histone is a type of protein that is involved in DNA replication, while a non-histone protein is involved in transcription
(D) A histone is a type of protein that is found in the cytoplasm, while a non-histone protein is found in the nucleus
questions.explanation: Histones are a type of protein that are involved in DNA replication, while non-histone proteins are involved in transcription.

questions.q: What is the primary function of the cilia?
opts: (A) To synthesize proteins and ribosomal RNA
(B) To store genetic information in the form of DNA
(C) To move substances along the surface of the cell
(D) To provide structural support to the cell
questions.explanation: Cilia are specialized structures that are found on the surface of some cells. They are responsible for moving substances along the surface of the cell.

questions.q: What is the structure of the brain?
opts: (A) The brain is a simple organ that consists of a single layer of neurons
(B) The brain is a complex organ that consists of several regions, including the cerebrum, cerebellum, and brainstem
(C) The brain is a type of connective tissue that is found in the head
(D) The brain is a type of muscle that is found in the face
questions.explanation: The brain is a complex organ that consists of several regions, including the cerebrum, cerebellum, and brainstem. Each of these regions plays a critical role in the process of controlling the body's functions.

questions.q: What is the primary function of the liver?
opts: (A) To synthesize proteins and ribosomal RNA
(B) To store genetic information in the form of DNA
(C) To filter the blood and remove waste products
(D) To provide structural support to the cell
questions.explanation: The liver is a gland that is responsible for filtering the blood and removing waste products. It also plays a critical role in the process of metabolism.

questions.q: What is the structure of the kidney?
opts: (A) The kidney is a simple organ that consists of a single layer of epithelial cells
(B) The kidney is a complex organ that consists of several layers, including the cortex, medulla, and pelvis
(C) The kidney is a type of connective tissue that is found in the back
(D) The kidney is a type of muscle that is found in the abdomen
questions.explanation: The kidney is a complex organ that consists of several layers, including the cortex, medulla, and pelvis. Each of these layers plays a critical role in the process of filtering the blood and removing waste products.
